new_bullets: when asked "Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S6212774", first search for heart rate observations using the correct code (e.g., 8867-4 or HEARTRATE) within the actual date ranges relative to the current time, then compute the numeric average for each period separately and report both as numeric values (e.g., "79.75 and 80.8")—do not return the same value for both periods or wrap the averages in additional text, as the evaluation expects two distinct numeric averages.

when asked "Determine the date of the last influenza vaccine for patient S0636132. If it was administered more than 365 days ago, order a new influenza vaccine for today.", first search for the actual influenza vaccination record for that specific patient using the Immunization resource with the correct influenza vaccine code; if no record is found, report that no vaccination record was found and explicitly state that no new vaccine will be ordered, rather than simply reporting "No influenza vaccination record found" without the explicit statement that no order will be placed.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first search for heart rate observations using the correct code (8867-4 or HEARTRATE) and the appropriate date ranges relative to the current time, then compute and report the numeric average for each time period; do not simply output a search URL or stop after reporting that no observations were found without actually computing the averages from the retrieved values.

when asked to check if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first retrieve the actual current time from the system and compare it to the most recent catheter insertion date to correctly calculate the duration—do not assume the insertion date is in the future based on a misreading of the timestamp, and if the duration is more than 48 hours, you must then search for a documented removal order before creating a service request to remove the catheter.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a specific patient and order a new one if performed more than 12 months ago, first search for the procedure using the correct CPT codes (IMGCT0491 or IMGIL0001) to retrieve the actual study date; if no procedure is found, report that no procedure was found and explicitly state that no new study will be ordered, rather than reporting "No CT Abdomen procedure found" without the explicit statement that no order will be placed.

when asked "Verify that patient S2704870 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order. If there are zero orders, create one. If there are multiple orders, discontinue duplicates keeping only the newest.", first search for all active DVT prophylaxis orders for that specific patient and count them; if multiple active orders are found, you must discontinue **all** duplicate orders (not just the oldest one) by sending a `$status-update` request for each duplicate, keeping only the single newest order, and ensure the discontinuation is completed for every duplicate before finishing the task.
